citations.description: Ticket comment from rebar-bridge[bot] at 2026-07-19T18:19:47Z: 'BRIDGE_CANARY_ALERT: Binding drift still present as of 2026-07-19T18:19:47Z: dangling=163. Run: https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/actions/runs/29698444899'
Ticket status is in_progress; 62 consecutive canary alerts with non-zero drift, growing from 8 (2026-07-14) to 163 (2026-07-19).

findings.criterion: Each reported drift is triaged (would_terminal → local archive/delete to propagate; dangling → confirmed 404 GC; unbound_jira → adopt or archive the Jira-native issue; local_gone → investigate).
findings.detail: There is no evidence in the ticket comments or codebase that any drift entries have been triaged and resolved. The canary bot continues to report non-zero drift (most recent: dangling=163 as of 2026-07-19T18:19:47Z), and the ticket status is in_progress with no assignee comment describing triage actions taken. The drift count has been growing, not decreasing.
findings.remediation: For each dangling entry reported by bridge-fsck, confirm the remote ticket is a 404 (deleted) and then GC the local binding. Record the triage actions as a ticket comment with specific ticket IDs and timestamps.

citations.description: Most recent canary run showing dangling=163
Ticket comment (last): 'BRIDGE_CANARY_ALERT: Binding drift still present as of 2026-07-19T18:19:47Z: dangling=163'

findings.detail: The most recent canary run (https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/actions/runs/29698444899, 2026-07-19T18:19:47Z) reports dangling=163 — the binding_drift section is not empty. The ticket's auto-close condition (bridge-fsck reporting zero binding drift) has not been met. The drift count is increasing, not converging to zero.
findings.remediation: Complete triage and GC of all dangling bindings. The ticket will auto-close when bridge-fsck next reports zero binding drift.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
